purpose: Playwright end-to-end coverage proving message delete (AC-DEL-1) and text edit (AC-EDIT-1) work through the real app in both DM and group threads, plus confirmatory coverage of the S6 UI gating (AC-AUTH-1, AC-DEL-6, AC-EDIT-3, AC-EDIT-5, AC-IMG-2) and S3's reconciliation behavior (AC-ORDER-1/3, AC-EDIT-7) that this story does not own but the epic's architecture.md 'E2E' bullet asks S7 to exercise as confirmatory coverage.
dependencies_allowed: data-testid attributes already emitted by ChatBox.tsx (msg-<id>, edited-marker-<id>, msg-edit-<id>, msg-delete-<id>, msg-delete-confirm-<id>, msg-delete-confirm-row-<id>, msg-delete-cancel-<id>, chat-edit-banner, chat-edit-cancel, chat-edit-empty-hint, chat-input, chat-send-btn, image-attachment-button, image-preview-thumbnail, image-thumbnail)
dependencies_forbidden: Direct imports of app/src/lib/messageEdits/api.ts or rumor.ts from test files (bridges/UI only, per the project's e2e-through-the-app rule)
Raw WebSocket signing/publishing of kind-5/9/14/1059 events
New production modules outside test-only data-testid additions
test_targets: AC-DEL-1: delete (text + image), DM and group
AC-AUTH-1 (confirmatory): action menu absent on peer message, present on own
AC-EDIT-5 (confirmatory): empty edit blocked with hint
AC-IMG-2 (confirmatory): image message offers delete, no edit affordance
AC-ORDER-1/AC-ORDER-3 (confirmatory, owned by S3): delete-before-original ordering never resurrects the message
AC-EDIT-7 (confirmatory, owned by S3): reactions survive an edit
AC-DEL-3 (confirmatory, owned by S3): reactions vanish on delete
notes: No production module is created or owned by this story. architecture.md's module map does not list an 'e2e' row (stories.json S7 note flags this transparently, mirroring the suggested-boundary list item 7). No new dependencies_allowed edge is introduced beyond what S1-S6 already exposed (dev bridges + testids), so there is nothing here that requires Architect escalation against architecture.md's module map.
